webhooks.name: mraycluster.ray.openshift.ai
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources to inject an OAuth proxy sidecar container for dashboard authentication and mTLS init containers, volumes, and environment variables for encrypted inter-node communication. The validating webhook ensures these injected components are not tampered with by users and blocks enableIngress from being set to true.

webhooks.name: vraycluster.ray.openshift.ai
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources to inject an OAuth proxy sidecar container for dashboard authentication and mTLS init containers, volumes, and environment variables for encrypted inter-node communication. The validating webhook ensures these injected components are not tampered with by users and blocks enableIngress from being set to true.

webhooks.name: clusterservingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update to ensure model format priorities are consistent (no duplicate priorities across runtimes for the same model format and protocol, and no conflicting priorities within a single runtime) and that multi-node WorkerSpec configurations are valid (e.g., disallowing removal of existing WorkerSpec, multiple containers, invalid parallel sizes, unknown GPU types, and forbidden environment variables).

webhooks.name: inferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.pod-mutator
webhooks.purpose: Mutates KServe-managed Pods at creation time by injecting storage initializer init-containers (to download model artifacts), credential/secret volume mounts for storage access, inference agent sidecars for request logging and batching, metrics aggregator sidecars, GKE accelerator node selectors, and Istio CNI security contexts. It reads the InferenceService config and the owning InferenceService to determine which sidecars and credentials to inject.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for LLMInferenceService v1alpha1 that enforces structural correctness on create and update: it rejects conflicting router/gateway/HTTPRoute configurations, validates parallelism constraints (data vs pipeline, required co-fields), ensures scheduler config has exactly one of inline or ref, delegates scaling validation to the v1alpha2 hub type, and blocks mutations to the immutable total parallelism size.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validating admission webhook for LLMInferenceService v1alpha2 resources on create and update. Enforces structural constraints including router/HTTPRoute cross-field consistency, parallelism configuration rules (pipeline vs data parallelism, worker requirements), immutability of parallelism size on updates, scheduler config completeness, and autoscaling rules such as HPA/KEDA mutual exclusivity, replica bounds, variantCost format, and actuator consistency between decode and prefill workloads.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update by forbidding the baseRefs field (recursive refs disabled), ensuring scheduler replicas are greater than zero, running optional injected validation logic, and emitting warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update, rejecting configs that use the forbidden baseRefs field or specify invalid scheduler replicas (must be >0), and emits warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update to ensure model format priorities are consistent (no duplicate priorities across runtimes for the same model format and protocol, and no conflicting priorities within a single runtime) and that multi-node WorkerSpec configurations are valid (e.g., disallowing removal of existing WorkerSpec, multiple containers, invalid parallel sizes, unknown GPU types, and forbidden environment variables).

webhooks.name: inferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.pod-mutator
webhooks.purpose: Mutates pods owned by KServe InferenceServices on creation, injecting storage-initializer init containers (with credentials for model download), inference-logger/batcher/metrics-aggregator agent sidecars, GKE GPU accelerator node selectors, and Istio CNI security contexts so that model-serving pods are fully configured before they start.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceService v1alpha1 resources on create and update, enforcing router/HTTPRoute cross-field constraints (e.g., refs vs inline spec, managed vs user-defined gateway compatibility), parallelism rules (data vs pipeline exclusivity, worker-requires-parallelism, positive values, data/dataLocal co-requirement), scheduler config integrity (replicas > 0, exactly one of inline or ref), workload scaling rules (delegated to v1alpha2), actuator backend consistency across decode and prefill workloads, and immutability of total parallelism size on updates.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceService resources on create and update, enforcing cross-field constraints on router/gateway/HTTPRoute configuration, parallelism settings (pipeline vs data parallelism mutual exclusivity, data/dataLocal co-requirement), immutability of total parallelism size, scheduler config completeness, and autoscaling rules including replica bounds, WVA actuator backend exclusivity (HPA vs KEDA), actuator consistency across decode and prefill workloads, variantCost format, and KEDA controller-owned field restrictions.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update, ensuring baseRefs are not set (recursive refs disabled), scheduler replicas are positive if specified, and emitting warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update by forbidding the baseRefs field (recursive refs are disabled), ensuring scheduler replicas are greater than zero, running any injected validation function, and emitting warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime create/update requests by ensuring model format priorities are consistent (no duplicate priorities across runtimes for the same model format and protocol, and same priorities within a single runtime for the same format), and validates multi-node WorkerSpec constraints including GPU resource types, parallel size values, blocked environment variables, and disallowed spec mutations.

webhooks.name: notebooks-validation.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates updates to Notebook resources, specifically preventing removal of the MLflow instance annotation while a notebook is running. This avoids a broken state where the MLflow RoleBinding would be deleted immediately but environment variables persist until pod restart, leaving the MLflow client with credentials but no permissions.

webhooks.name: notebooks.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook for Kubeflow Notebook CRs that enriches notebook pods on create/update by injecting a kube-rbac-proxy authentication sidecar, mounting trusted CA certificate bundles, resolving container images from OpenShift ImageStreams, mounting pipeline runtime configuration (ConfigMap and Elyra Secret), injecting cluster-wide proxy environment variables, setting MLflow tracking environment variables, and preventing unintended pod restarts by blocking webhook-only spec changes on running notebooks until they are stopped or restarted.

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources on create/update to enforce platform-specific network security defaults. On OpenShift clusters (detected via Route API availability), it sets the secure trusted network annotation to true and forcibly disables enableIngress to enforce Gateway API-only access with centralized authentication; on non-OpenShift clusters, it sets the annotation to false.

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources on create/update to enforce platform-specific network security defaults. On OpenShift clusters (detected via Route API availability), it sets the secure trusted network annotation to true and forcibly disables enableIngress to enforce Gateway API-only access with centralized authentication; on non-OpenShift clusters, it sets the annotation to false.

webhooks.name: vraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates RayCluster resources on create and update, ensuring the cluster name matches a DNS-compatible regex pattern (lowercase alphanumeric, starting with a letter) and that all worker group names within the spec are unique.

webhooks.name: vcohort.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates Cohort resources on create and update by checking the spec's fairSharing and resourceGroups fields against validation rules, rejecting invalid configurations with a fail policy. It performs purely structural validation on the submitted object without fetching any external state.

webhooks.name: vogxserver.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for OGXServer custom resources on create and update. It enforces that the distribution name is known, provider IDs are unique, model-to-provider references resolve to configured providers, and adoption annotations do not self-reference the CR name (which would cause Deployment name conflicts).

webhooks.name: mmodelregistry.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook applies default values to ModelRegistry CRs on create/update, while validating webhook ensures registry spec correctness (database config, fields) and enforces cluster-wide name uniqueness by listing all existing ModelRegistry resources on create.

webhooks.name: vmodelregistry.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook applies default values to ModelRegistry CRs on create/update, while validating webhook ensures registry spec correctness (database config, fields) and enforces cluster-wide name uniqueness by listing all existing ModelRegistry resources on create.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.modelmesh-webhook-server.default
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources that enforces autoscaler configuration rules on multi-model (ModelMesh-compatible) runtimes. It rejects creates/updates when the autoscaler class is unsupported, the target utilization percentage is outside 1-100, HPA metrics are invalid, or both an autoscaler and explicit replicas are set simultaneously.

webhooks.name: minferencegraph-v1alpha1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates InferenceGraph resources on create/update to ensure that serviceUrl fields in graph node steps reference only cluster-internal services within the same namespace as the InferenceGraph, preventing cross-namespace references and public hostname usage for security (e.g., preventing leakage of Authorization headers). The mutating webhook is a no-op defaulter.

webhooks.name: minferenceservice-v1beta1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates that new InferenceService resources have names within the allowed length and are not created in the protected applications namespace (determined by the DSCInitialization CR), and provides a no-op mutating defaulter scaffold for future defaulting logic.

webhooks.name: mutating.pod.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates newly created Pods that have the RAY_USE_TLS environment variable enabled on their kserve-container or worker-container by injecting TLS-related volumes (an emptyDir and a Secret volume), volume mounts, and an init container that copies Ray TLS certificates from a Kubernetes Secret into the pod so Ray cluster communication is encrypted.

webhooks.name: validating.isvc.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates that new InferenceService resources have names within the allowed length and are not created in the protected applications namespace (determined by the DSCInitialization CR), and provides a no-op mutating defaulter scaffold for future defaulting logic.

webhooks.name: validating.nim.account.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates NIM Account creation by enforcing a singleton constraint: only one Account resource is allowed per namespace. Updates and deletions are always permitted without validation.

webhooks.name: vinferencegraph-v1alpha1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates InferenceGraph resources on create/update to ensure that serviceUrl fields in graph node steps reference only cluster-internal services within the same namespace as the InferenceGraph, preventing cross-namespace references and public hostname usage for security (e.g., preventing leakage of Authorization headers). The mutating webhook is a no-op defaulter.

webhooks.name: vogxserver.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for OGXServer custom resources on create and update operations. It enforces that the distribution name is known, provider IDs are unique, model-to-provider references resolve to configured providers, and adoption annotations do not equal the CR name (which would cause Deployment name conflicts).

webhooks.name: mutate-pod.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook that intercepts Pod create/update requests in Spark job namespaces, looks up the owning SparkApplication, and defaults the pod spec with settings from the SparkApplication CR—including volumes, env vars, config maps, affinity, tolerations, node selectors, security contexts, GPU resources, sidecar/init containers, ports, scheduling, and owner references—differentiating between driver and executor pods.

webhooks.name: mutate-sparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default values on SparkApplication resources during create and update operations. It only applies defaults when the application is in a 'New' or 'Invalidating' state, delegating to the operator's scheme-registered defaulting logic to populate unset fields with sensible defaults.

webhooks.name: validate-scheduledsparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates ScheduledSparkApplication resources on create and update, ensuring the metadata.name is a valid DNS-1035 label so that downstream Kubernetes Services and child SparkApplication resources can be created without naming failures. The core validation logic is currently a stub (TODO).

webhooks.name: validate-sparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: A mutating admission webhook defaulter for ScheduledSparkApplication resources. Currently a no-op placeholder that logs when a ScheduledSparkApplication is created or updated, but does not yet set any default field values.

webhooks.name: dscinitialization-v2-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Enforces DSCInitialization v2 as a singleton resource (denies creation if one already exists) and prevents deletion while any DataScienceCluster object still exists, ensuring the initialization resource outlives all clusters it supports.

webhooks.name: connection-notebook.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates and injects data-science connection secrets into Kubeflow Notebook containers. On create/update, it parses the opendatahub.io/connections annotation for secret references, verifies each secret exists and the requesting user has 'get' permission via SubjectAccessReview, then mutates the notebook pod spec by adding or removing the referenced secrets as envFrom entries on the first container.

webhooks.name: connection-llmisvc.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates LLMInferenceService resources on create/update to inject data-connection details (model URI, imagePullSecrets, serviceAccountName) from a referenced Secret, and cleans up those injections when the connection annotation is removed or changed.

webhooks.name: dscinitialization-v1-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Enforces singleton semantics for DSCInitialization v1 resources by denying creation when one already exists, and prevents deletion of DSCInitialization while any DataScienceCluster object still exists in the cluster.

webhooks.name: connection-isvc.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for KServe InferenceService resources that automatically injects model-serving connection details (storage credentials, URIs, service accounts) into the ISVC spec based on a platform connection annotation, supporting S3, OCI, and URI connection types with full lifecycle management (inject on create, replace/remove on update).

webhooks.name: hardwareprofile-notebook-injector.opendatahub.io
webhooks.enable_condition: Kserve component enabled OR Workbenches component enabled
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook that intercepts create/update of Notebooks, InferenceServices, and LLMInferenceServices to inject hardware profile specifications (resource requirements, tolerations, nodeSelector) into workload containers based on the referenced HardwareProfile CR identified by the opendatahub.io/hardware-profile-name annotation. It also handles cleanup of previously-injected settings when the annotation is removed or when switching between profiles, preserving any manually-added configuration.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v2-defaulter.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default field values on DataScienceCluster v2 resources during create and update. Specifically, when ModelRegistry is Managed and RegistriesNamespace is empty, it defaults it to the standard namespace.

webhooks.name: podmonitor-injector.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that automatically injects the opendatahub.io/monitoring=true label onto PodMonitor and ServiceMonitor resources created or updated in namespaces labeled for ODH monitoring, after verifying that the cluster-scoped Monitoring CR exists (i.e., monitoring is enabled).

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v2-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DataScienceCluster v2 create and update requests. On create, it enforces a singleton constraint (denying creation if an instance already exists) and rejects any DSC where Kueue's managementState is set to Managed. On update, it only enforces the Kueue managementState restriction.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v1-defaulter.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that defaults fields on DataScienceCluster v1 resources during create and update. Specifically, when the ModelRegistry component is Managed and RegistriesNamespace is empty, it sets RegistriesNamespace to the default value.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v1-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DataScienceCluster v1 resources on create and update. Enforces singleton creation (only one DSC instance allowed), denies Kueue with ManagementState=Managed, and prevents v1 API updates when v2-only components are currently Managed to avoid silent data loss during v1-to-v2 conversion.

webhooks.name: vworkspace.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for Workspace resources on create and update. It fetches the referenced WorkspaceKind to verify that the Workspace's selected imageConfig and podConfig IDs exist in the WorkspaceKind's allowed values, and validates pod metadata labels and annotations.

webhooks.name: vworkspacekind.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for WorkspaceKind resources that enforces structural correctness on create (pod metadata, env vars, imageConfig/podConfig values, redirects, defaults) and update (incremental validation of changed configs, redirect cycle detection, preventing removal of in-use options by lazily counting Workspace usage), and blocks deletion when the WorkspaceKind is still referenced by existing Workspaces or protected by a finalizer.

webhooks.name: localmodelnamespacecache.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LocalModelNamespaceCache resources on create, update, and delete. On create/update, it ensures all referenced node groups exist as LocalModelNodeGroup resources. On delete, it prevents removal if any InferenceService in the namespace still references the cache via its labels.

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates and validates RayCluster (ray.io/v1) resources for Kueue integration. The mutating webhook applies defaults for local queue assignment, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue, and sets managed-by labels. The validating webhook ensures Kueue-managed RayClusters do not use in-tree autoscaling, have at most 7 worker groups, do not use the reserved "head" group name for workers, and carry valid topology-aware scheduling annotations.

webhooks.name: vraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates and validates RayCluster (ray.io/v1) resources for Kueue integration. The mutating webhook applies defaults for local queue assignment, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue, and sets managed-by labels. The validating webhook ensures Kueue-managed RayClusters do not use in-tree autoscaling, have at most 7 worker groups, do not use the reserved "head" group name for workers, and carry valid topology-aware scheduling annotations.

webhooks.name: mresourceflavor.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Defaults and validates Kueue ResourceFlavor objects: the mutating webhook adds a ResourceInUse finalizer on creation, while the validating webhook ensures nodeLabels, nodeTaints (unique by key+effect), and tolerations are well-formed on create and update.

webhooks.name: vresourceflavor.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Defaults and validates Kueue ResourceFlavor objects: the mutating webhook adds a ResourceInUse finalizer on creation, while the validating webhook ensures nodeLabels, nodeTaints (unique by key+effect), and tolerations are well-formed on create and update.

webhooks.name: mclusterqueue.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for Kueue ClusterQueue resources. The mutating webhook adds a ResourceInUse finalizer on creation, while the validating webhook enforces structural correctness of resource groups, flavor quotas, namespace selectors, admission check configuration, preemption policies, fair sharing settings, and borrowing/lending limits (e.g., lending limit must not exceed nominal quota, and limits must be nil when the queue has no cohort).

webhooks.name: vclusterqueue.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for Kueue ClusterQueue resources. The mutating webhook adds a ResourceInUse finalizer on creation, while the validating webhook enforces structural correctness of resource groups, flavor quotas, namespace selectors, admission check configuration, preemption policies, fair sharing settings, and borrowing/lending limits (e.g., lending limit must not exceed nominal quota, and limits must be nil when the queue has no cohort).

webhooks.name: mworkload.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Defaults and validates Kueue Workload resources: the mutating webhook strips minCount from pod sets when the PartialAdmission feature gate is disabled, while the validating webhook enforces constraints on create and update including at most one variable-count pod set, valid admission pod-set assignments, immutable admission and pod-set fields once quota is reserved, and non-decreasing reclaimable pod counts.

webhooks.name: vworkload.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Defaults and validates Kueue Workload resources: the mutating webhook strips minCount from pod sets when the PartialAdmission feature gate is disabled, while the validating webhook enforces constraints on create and update including at most one variable-count pod set, valid admission pod-set assignments, immutable admission and pod-set fields once quota is reserved, and non-decreasing reclaimable pod counts.

webhooks.name: mjobset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating admission webhook for JobSet resources managed by Kueue. The mutating webhook (mjobset.kb.io) applies defaults on create: assigns a default local queue, suspends the JobSet if it should be managed by Kueue (checking the owner chain to avoid double-managing), and sets the managedBy field. The validating webhook (vjobset.kb.io) enforces Kueue invariants on create and update, including queue-name immutability, label consistency, and topology-aware scheduling (TAS) pod-set annotation validity for each replicated job.

webhooks.name: vjobset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating admission webhook for JobSet resources managed by Kueue. The mutating webhook (mjobset.kb.io) applies defaults on create: assigns a default local queue, suspends the JobSet if it should be managed by Kueue (checking the owner chain to avoid double-managing), and sets the managedBy field. The validating webhook (vjobset.kb.io) enforces Kueue invariants on create and update, including queue-name immutability, label consistency, and topology-aware scheduling (TAS) pod-set annotation validity for each replicated job.

webhooks.name: mstatefulset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates and validates StatefulSet resources for Kueue queue management. The mutating webhook propagates queue-name labels and pod-group annotations to pod templates and marks StatefulSets for suspension when managed by Kueue. The validating webhook enforces queue-name immutability while replicas are running, prevents pod spec and replica count changes (except scale to/from zero) for Kueue-managed StatefulSets, and validates priority class updates.

webhooks.name: vstatefulset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates and validates StatefulSet resources for Kueue queue management. The mutating webhook propagates queue-name labels and pod-group annotations to pod templates and marks StatefulSets for suspension when managed by Kueue. The validating webhook enforces queue-name immutability while replicas are running, prevents pod spec and replica count changes (except scale to/from zero) for Kueue-managed StatefulSets, and validates priority class updates.

webhooks.name: mleaderworkerset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for LeaderWorkerSet resources managed by Kueue. The mutating webhook applies default queue labels, sets workload priority class labels on pod templates, and injects suspension annotations so that Kueue can control scheduling. The validating webhook ensures the queue name label is valid and immutable across updates, validates topology-aware scheduling annotations, validates workload priority class immutability, and prevents changes to pod template specs while the workload is Kueue-managed.

webhooks.name: vleaderworkerset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for LeaderWorkerSet resources managed by Kueue. The mutating webhook applies default queue labels, sets workload priority class labels on pod templates, and injects suspension annotations so that Kueue can control scheduling. The validating webhook ensures the queue name label is valid and immutable across updates, validates topology-aware scheduling annotations, validates workload priority class immutability, and prevents changes to pod template specs while the workload is Kueue-managed.

webhooks.name: mrayjob.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates incoming RayJob resources to apply Kueue defaults (local queue name, suspension for admission control, managed-by annotation) and validates that Kueue-managed RayJobs are properly configured: they must delete the cluster after finishing, must not reference an existing cluster or use autoscaling, are limited to 7 worker groups with no group named "head", and must have valid topology-aware scheduling requests.

webhooks.name: vrayjob.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates incoming RayJob resources to apply Kueue defaults (local queue name, suspension for admission control, managed-by annotation) and validates that Kueue-managed RayJobs are properly configured: they must delete the cluster after finishing, must not reference an existing cluster or use autoscaling, are limited to 7 worker groups with no group named "head", and must have valid topology-aware scheduling requests.

webhooks.name: mmpijob.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating and validating admission webhooks for Kubeflow MPIJob resources managed by Kueue. The mutating webhook defaults the local queue name, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue (checking ancestor ownership and namespace selectors), and applies managed-by defaults. The validating webhook enforces Kueue job constraints on create/update and validates Topology-Aware Scheduling annotations on each MPI replica spec.

webhooks.name: vmpijob.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating and validating admission webhooks for Kubeflow MPIJob resources managed by Kueue. The mutating webhook defaults the local queue name, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue (checking ancestor ownership and namespace selectors), and applies managed-by defaults. The validating webhook enforces Kueue job constraints on create/update and validates Topology-Aware Scheduling annotations on each MPI replica spec.

webhooks.name: validator.pytorchjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates PyTorchJob resources on create and update, enforcing naming conventions (DNS-1035), run policy constraints, replica spec requirements (valid replica types of Master/Worker, exactly 1 master replica, at least 1 worker replica, a container named 'pytorch' with an image), and elastic policy rules (workers required when elastic policy is set, nProcPerNode deprecation).

webhooks.name: validator.jaxjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates JAXJob resources on create and update, ensuring the object name is a valid DNS-1035 label, that jaxReplicaSpecs are present and only use the 'Worker' replica type, that each replica spec has containers with images defined, and that at least one container is named 'jax'.

webhooks.name: validator.xgboostjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates XGBoostJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is DNS-1035 compliant, replica specs contain only valid types (Master/Worker), exactly one Master replica exists, every container has an image defined, and a default container named 'xgboost' is present in each replica spec.

webhooks.name: validator.paddlejob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates PaddleJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is DNS-1035 compliant, replica specs contain only valid replica types (Master, Worker), each replica has containers with images defined, and at least one container is named 'paddle'. It also validates the RunPolicy and prevents invalid RunPolicy updates.

webhooks.name: validator.tfjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TFJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, each replica spec contains at least one container with a defined image and a container named 'tensorflow', and that no more than one Chief or Master replica role is specified.

webhooks.name: validator.clustertrainingruntime.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates ClusterTrainingRuntime resources on create and update by ensuring their replicated job specifications are well-formed, and emits a deprecation warning on create if the runtime is labeled as deprecated.

webhooks.name: validator.trainjob.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TrainJob resources on create and update by resolving the referenced runtime (from spec.runtimeRef) and delegating to its ValidateObjects method, rejecting requests that reference an unsupported runtime or fail runtime-specific validation rules.

webhooks.name: validator.trainingruntime.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TrainingRuntime resources on create to ensure that replicated jobs with ancestor labels (trainer, model-initializer, dataset-initializer) have exactly 1 replica and contain the required container name corresponding to their ancestor type (e.g., 'node' for trainer, 'model-initializer' for model-initializer).

webhooks.name: localmodelnamespacecache.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LocalModelNamespaceCache resources on create, update, and delete. On create/update, it ensures all referenced node groups exist; on delete, it prevents removal if any InferenceService in the namespace still references the cache.
